title: Continuity vs Uniform Continuity in Metric Spaces
description: A flowchart comparing the definitions and properties of continuous and uniformly continuous functions between metric spaces.
relationships: f is continuous at x₀ if for every ε > 0, there exists δ > 0 (depending on ε AND x₀) such that if d_X(x, x₀) < δ then d_Y(f(x), f(x₀)) < ε
f is uniformly continuous on X if for every ε > 0, there exists δ > 0 (depending only on ε) such that for all x, y ∈ X, if d_X(x, y) < δ then d_Y(f(x), f(y)) < ε
Continuity does not necessarily imply uniform continuity (e.g., f(x)=1/x on (0,1))
